title: Venezuelan Corn Empanadas
description: Salty, crunchy, and energizing, these empanadas are a delicious Venezuelan dish featuring oat milk and tadka, making them flexitarian-friendly. They are perfect for a quick snack or lunch.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine cornmeal, flour, salt, sugar, and yeast.
Gradually add oat milk, vegetable oil, and water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and brush with tadka spice mix.
Place the empanadas in the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Serve the empanadas warm and enjoy.
